issues.title: discovery.md mermaid drops the checkmark from 'verified' label vs the plan's intended design
issues.description: The plan's intended-design diagram (plan.md) renders the 'code confirms' outcome node as `mark verified \u2713 \u00b7 raise Confidence` (with a Unicode checkmark \u2713). The shipped `docs/discovery.md` mermaid (line 21) reads `mark verified \u00b7 raise Confidence` — the checkmark was dropped. The diagram still renders and is functionally correct; this is a cosmetic deviation from the intended design. The checkmark makes the 'verified' outcome visually distinct from 'corrected' and 'unverifiable' at a glance.
issues.proposed_solution: In docs/discovery.md line 21, change `OK["mark verified \u00b7 raise Confidence"]` to `OK["mark verified \u2713 \u00b7 raise Confidence"]` to match the plan's intended visual design. (AGENT-FIXABLE — if the checkmark causes rendering issues in just-the-docs/mermaid 10.9.1, mark wontfix instead.)
issues.fix_summary: Restored the Unicode checkmark in docs/discovery.md line 21: now 'mark verified ✓ · raise Confidence', matching the plan's intended-design diagram and the charts .mmd/viewer. Renders fine in mermaid.
